title: log: Mitigate disk filling attacks by rate limiting LogPrintf, LogInfo, LogWarning, LogError
summary: Implements rate limiting for Bitcoin Core's logging functions to prevent attackers from filling up disk space through excessive log generation.
potential_rationale: This likely helps protect Bitcoin nodes from denial-of-service attacks where malicious actors could trigger excessive logging to consume all available disk space, which could cause the node to crash or become unusable.

title: log: rate limiting followups
summary: Makes follow-up improvements to the log rate limiting feature, including consistent prefix marking and disabling rate limiting in tests.
potential_rationale: This appears to refine the rate limiting implementation to ensure better user experience by making rate-limited logs more identifiable and preventing test failures that could occur due to unexpected log suppression during automated testing.

title: test: modify logging_filesize_rate_limit params
summary: Adjusts parameters in logging rate limit tests to use a longer time window and fewer log lines for more reliable test execution.
potential_rationale: This likely improves test reliability by reducing the chance of race conditions or timing-related test failures, which may help ensure the rate limiting functionality works correctly across different testing environments.

title: net: check for empty header before calling FillBlock
summary: Adds a check to prevent crashes when multiple compact block transaction messages are received by validating headers before processing.
potential_rationale: This appears to fix a potential crash vulnerability in the P2P networking code that could be exploited by malicious peers sending multiple compact block messages, which may help improve node stability and security.

title: fuzz: compact block harness
summary: Adds a new fuzzing test harness specifically designed to test compact block functionality for finding potential bugs.
potential_rationale: This likely helps improve Bitcoin Core's security and reliability by enabling automated testing tools to discover edge cases and potential vulnerabilities in the compact block implementation, which may prevent consensus bugs or crashes.

title: test: change log rate limit version gate
summary: This PR adjusts a version gate in the test framework from 299900 to 290100 to bypass log rate limiting when explicit versions are set. It appears to be a minor test infrastructure adjustment.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves test reliability and debugging capabilities by ensuring that log rate limiting doesn't interfere with test execution when specific Bitcoin Core versions are being tested. This may help developers better diagnose issues during functional testing.

title: net: fix use-after-free with v2->v1 reconnection logic
summary: This PR fixes a use-after-free memory bug in the connection manager that occurs during v2-to-v1 reconnection scenarios. The fix clears reconnection data structures when the connection manager stops to prevent accessing freed memory.
potential_rationale: This appears to be a critical stability fix that likely prevents potential crashes or undefined behavior in Bitcoin Core's networking layer. Use-after-free bugs can lead to node instability and may create security vulnerabilities, so fixing this likely improves the overall reliability of peer-to-peer connections.
